skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to replace a large fixed upfront server purchase with an expense you pay only as you consume capacity. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: Pay-as-you-go pricing is correct because Pay-as-you-go converts a fixed capital expense into a variable operating expense billed only for what you use. Distractors: Economies of scale lowers prices as aggregated usage grows. Elasticity scales resources up and down to match demand. Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions worldwide quickly.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: A design review identifies this requirement: get progressively lower prices because AWS aggregates usage from hundreds of thousands of customers. What should you configure?
explanation: Economies of scale is correct because Aggregated demand lets AWS achieve economies of scale and pass lower pay-as-you-go prices to customers. Distractors: Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions worldwide quickly. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) compares full on-premises versus cloud cost. Elasticity scales resources up and down to match demand.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to add and remove capacity automatically so you never pay for idle servers or run short at peak?
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
explanation: Elasticity is correct because Elasticity acquires resources when needed and releases them when not, matching capacity to demand. Distractors: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions worldwide quickly. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) compares full on-premises versus cloud cost.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: launch the same application in data centers on other continents within minutes; and provision a new test environment in minutes instead of waiting weeks for hardware.
options: Pay only for what you use
explanation: Go global in minutes is correct: AWS global infrastructure lets you deploy to multiple Regions worldwide quickly for lower latency to users. Increased speed and agility is correct: In the cloud new resources are a click away, cutting provisioning time from weeks to minutes. Pay only for what you use is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Pay-as-you-go pricing bills only for what you consume, with no upfront commitment. Economies of scale lowers prices as aggregated usage grows.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: An operations team wants to compare the complete cost of on-premises servers (power, cooling, staff, real estate) against AWS. Which choice is most appropriate?
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
explanation: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) is correct because TCO compares all direct and indirect costs; AWS removes data center, hardware, power, and administration overhead. Distractors: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Increased speed and agility makes new resources available in minutes. Stop guessing capacity removes the need to forecast capacity in advance.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: During implementation, you must let engineers stop racking, powering, and cooling servers so they work on the product instead. Which AWS feature should you choose?
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
explanation: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting is correct because AWS removes the undifferentiated work of running infrastructure so teams focus on what differentiates the business. Distractors: Stop guessing capacity removes the need to forecast capacity in advance. Operating expense (OpEx) model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Increased speed and agility makes new resources available in minutes.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: An organization has the following requirement: provision a new test environment in minutes instead of waiting weeks for hardware. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: Increased speed and agility is correct because In the cloud new resources are a click away, cutting provisioning time from weeks to minutes. Distractors: AWS price reductions over time is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Stop guessing capacity removes the need to forecast capacity in advance. Operating expense (OpEx) model is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: avoid buying too much or too little hardware by no longer forecasting capacity before launch; and pay nothing for the servers you turn off overnight in a development account?
options: Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting
Pay only for what you use
explanation: Stop guessing capacity is correct: You scale on demand rather than guessing infrastructure capacity needs in advance, avoiding waste or shortfalls. Pay only for what you use is correct: Because billing is consumption-based, stopping or deleting resources stops the charges for them. Go global in minutes deploys to multiple Regions worldwide quickly. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) compares full on-premises versus cloud cost. Stop spending on undifferentiated heavy lifting is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: What should be configured first when the goal is to treat infrastructure as a monthly operating cost rather than a depreciating asset you own?
options: Pay only for what you use
explanation: Operating expense (OpEx) model is correct because The cloud shifts spending from capital expense on owned assets to operating expense for consumed services. Distractors: AWS price reductions over time is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Pay only for what you use is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Pay-as-you-go pricing bills only for what you consume, with no upfront commitment.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: Your team needs to benefit when AWS lowers prices over time as it grows and becomes more efficient. Which AWS option should you use?
options: Pay only for what you use
explanation: AWS price reductions over time is correct because AWS has repeatedly reduced prices as scale and efficiency improve, a recurring economic benefit of the cloud. Distractors: Pay-as-you-go pricing bills only for what you consume, with no upfront commitment. Economies of scale lowers prices as aggregated usage grows. Pay only for what you use is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Define the value and economics of the AWS Cloud
question: To pay nothing for the servers you turn off overnight in a development account, which AWS capability should you choose?
options: Pay only for what you use
explanation: Pay only for what you use is correct because Because billing is consumption-based, stopping or deleting resources stops the charges for them. question: A workload requires that you review a workload against six pillars of best practice before and after it goes live. What is the correct choice?
explanation: AWS Well-Architected Framework is correct because The Well-Architected Framework provides six pillars and best practices to review and improve workloads. Distractors: Design for failure / build redundancy removes single points of failure. Loose coupling reduces dependencies between components. Automation replaces manual steps with repeatable code.

question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to assume any component can fail and remove single points of failure so the system keeps running. What do you select?
explanation: Design for failure / build redundancy is correct because Designing for failure and adding redundancy eliminates single points of failure so the system tolerates component loss. Distractors: Loose coupling reduces dependencies between components. Automation replaces manual steps with repeatable code. Operational Excellence pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: Which AWS feature lets you reduce dependencies between components so they can be changed, scaled, or fail independently?
explanation: Loose coupling is correct because Loose coupling reduces interdependencies through well-defined interfaces so components evolve and fail independently. Distractors: Operational Excellence pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Security pillar covers identity, detection, and data protection. Automation replaces manual steps with repeatable code.

question: Choose the two AWS capabilities that meet these needs: replace manual, error-prone setup steps with repeatable code; and ensure a workload performs its intended function correctly and recovers from failure.
explanation: Automation is correct: Automating changes with code reduces human error and makes deployments consistent and repeatable. Reliability pillar is correct: The Reliability pillar focuses on recovering from failures and meeting demand with correct, consistent operation. AWS Well-Architected Framework is a review framework of pillars, not a single design action. Design for failure / build redundancy removes single points of failure. Loose coupling reduces dependencies between components.

question: Which option should you implement to operate workloads to deliver business value and continuously improve processes?
explanation: Operational Excellence pillar is correct because The Operational Excellence pillar focuses on running and monitoring systems and continuously improving processes. Distractors: Security pillar covers identity, detection, and data protection. Reliability pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Performance Efficiency pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: You need a solution to protect information, systems, and assets through identity, detection, and data protection. Which option fits best?
explanation: Security pillar is correct because The Security pillar covers identity and access, detection, infrastructure and data protection, and incident response. Distractors: Reliability pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Performance Efficiency pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Cost Optimization pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to ensure a workload performs its intended function correctly and recovers from failure. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: Reliability pillar is correct because The Reliability pillar focuses on recovering from failures and meeting demand with correct, consistent operation. Distractors: Cost Optimization pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Sustainability pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Performance Efficiency pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: Two requirements must be met: use computing resources efficiently and keep using them efficiently as demand changes; and review a workload against six pillars of best practice before and after it goes live. Select the two correct options.
explanation: Performance Efficiency pillar is correct: The Performance Efficiency pillar focuses on using resources efficiently and maintaining that as needs evolve. AWS Well-Architected Framework is correct: The Well-Architected Framework provides six pillars and best practices to review and improve workloads. Security pillar covers identity, detection, and data protection. Reliability pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Cost Optimization pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: A design review identifies this requirement: avoid unnecessary cost and understand where money is being spent. What should you configure?
explanation: Cost Optimization pillar is correct because The Cost Optimization pillar focuses on avoiding unneeded cost and getting the best value for spend. Distractors: Sustainability pillar is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Well-Architected Framework is a review framework of pillars, not a single design action. Design for failure / build redundancy removes single points of failure.

question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to minimize the environmental impact and energy use of running cloud workloads?
explanation: Sustainability pillar is correct because The Sustainability pillar focuses on reducing energy consumption and improving efficiency across the workload. Distractors: AWS Well-Architected Framework is a review framework of pillars, not a single design action. Design for failure / build redundancy removes single points of failure. Loose coupling reduces dependencies between components.

question: An operations team wants to organize a cloud adoption program across business, people, governance, platform, security, and operations. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) is correct because The AWS CAF organizes adoption guidance into six perspectives to build and run a cloud adoption plan. Distractors: Replatform makes small cloud optimizations during migration. Repurchase switches to a different product such as SaaS. Rehost lifts and shifts with minimal change.

question: During implementation, you must move an application to AWS with minimal changes by lifting and shifting it. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: Rehost is correct because Rehosting (lift and shift) moves an application to the cloud without changing its architecture, the fastest strategy. Distractors: AWS DataSync moves large datasets to AWS online at high speed. Replatform makes small cloud optimizations during migration. Repurchase switches to a different product such as SaaS.

question: An organization has the following requirement: move to a managed database during migration with a few optimizations but no rewrite. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: Replatform is correct because Replatforming (lift, tinker, and shift) makes small cloud optimizations such as a managed database without re-architecting. Distractors: Repurchase switches to a different product such as SaaS. AWS DataSync moves large datasets to AWS online at high speed. AWS Migration Hub tracks migration progress across tools.

question: Pick the two features that address these requirements: retire the existing product and switch to a different one, often SaaS; and decommission applications that are no longer useful instead of migrating them.
explanation: Repurchase is correct: Repurchasing replaces the current product with a different one, frequently a SaaS offering. Retire is correct: Retiring shuts down applications that are no longer needed, reducing cost and attack surface. AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) organizes adoption across perspectives, not a single migration. Rehost lifts and shifts with minimal change. Replatform makes small cloud optimizations during migration.

question: What should be configured first when the goal is to transfer large datasets to AWS over the network with a managed high-speed service?
explanation: AWS DataSync is correct because AWS DataSync moves large amounts of data online between on-premises storage and AWS at high speed. Distractors: Retire is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Retain is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Migration Hub tracks migration progress across tools.

question: Your team needs to track the status of servers and applications as they migrate to AWS in one place. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: AWS Migration Hub is correct because AWS Migration Hub gives a single place to track migration progress across AWS and partner tools. Distractors: Refactor is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Retire is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Retain is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: To decommission applications that are no longer useful instead of migrating them, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Retire is correct because Retiring shuts down applications that are no longer needed, reducing cost and attack surface. Distractors: Retain is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Refactor is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Application Migration Service is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: keep some applications on premises for now and revisit migrating them later; and organize a cloud adoption program across business, people, governance, platform, security, and operations.
explanation: Retain is correct: Retaining keeps applications where they are, for example when there is no current business case to migrate. AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) is correct: The AWS CAF organizes adoption guidance into six perspectives to build and run a cloud adoption plan. AWS Migration Hub tracks migration progress across tools. Retire is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Refactor is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: A workload requires that you rework an application to be cloud-native, for example breaking it into microservices. What is the correct choice?
explanation: Refactor is correct because Refactoring (re-architecting) significantly changes the application to take full advantage of cloud-native features. Distractors: AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) organizes adoption across perspectives, not a single migration. Rehost lifts and shifts with minimal change. AWS Application Migration Service is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to automate lift-and-shift migration of many servers while they keep running. What do you select?
explanation: AWS Application Migration Service is correct because AWS Application Migration Service automates rehosting of servers to AWS with minimal downtime. Distractors: Replatform makes small cloud optimizations during migration. AWS Cloud Adoption Framework (CAF) organizes adoption across perspectives, not a single migration. Rehost lifts and shifts with minimal change.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which AWS feature lets you decide who installs operating system patches on an Amazon EC2 instance?
options: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
AWS provides physical data center security
Customer configures IAM users and permissions
explanation: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is correct because For EC2 the customer manages the guest operating system, including its patches; AWS manages the host and hypervisor. Distractors: AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is an AWS responsibility, not the customer's. AWS provides physical data center security is always an AWS responsibility. Customer configures IAM users and permissions is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which option should you implement to decide who maintains the host hypervisor and the physical servers?
options: AWS provides physical data center security
Customer configures IAM users and permissions
Customer classifies and encrypts its data
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
explanation: AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is correct because AWS is responsible for the infrastructure that runs services, including the hypervisor and physical hardware. Distractors: AWS provides physical data center security is always an AWS responsibility. Customer configures IAM users and permissions is a customer responsibility. Customer classifies and encrypts its data is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: You need a solution to decide who is responsible for the physical security of AWS data centers. Which option fits best?
options: Customer classifies and encrypts its data
AWS provides physical data center security
Customer configures IAM users and permissions
explanation: AWS provides physical data center security is correct because Physical and environmental security of AWS facilities is always an AWS responsibility. Distractors: Customer classifies and encrypts its data is a customer responsibility. AWS operates managed-service infrastructure is AWS-managed for managed services. Customer configures IAM users and permissions is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: decide who configures IAM users, roles, and permissions in the account; and decide who configures security groups and network traffic rules around an EC2 instance?
options: Customer configures IAM users and permissions
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
AWS provides physical data center security
Customer classifies and encrypts its data
explanation: Customer configures IAM users and permissions is correct: Identity and access management configuration is a customer responsibility in the shared responsibility model. Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is correct: Configuring network controls such as security groups around customer resources is a customer responsibility. AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is an AWS responsibility, not the customer's. AWS provides physical data center security is always an AWS responsibility. Customer classifies and encrypts its data is a customer responsibility.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to decide who classifies and encrypts the customer's own data. Which option best meets the requirement?
options: Customer classifies and encrypts its data
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
explanation: Customer classifies and encrypts its data is correct because Customers always own their data and decide how it is classified and encrypted, and who can access it. Distractors: AWS operates managed-service infrastructure is AWS-managed for managed services. Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS maintains the global network and Regions is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: A design review identifies this requirement: decide who operates the underlying servers and OS for a managed service like Amazon S3. What should you configure?
options: Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
Customer manages applications on its instances
explanation: AWS operates managed-service infrastructure is correct because For managed services AWS operates the infrastructure, OS, and platform, so customers focus on data and access. Distractors: Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS maintains the global network and Regions is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Customer manages applications on its instances is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to decide who configures security groups and network traffic rules around an EC2 instance?
options: Customer manages applications on its instances
Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
explanation: Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is correct because Configuring network controls such as security groups around customer resources is a customer responsibility. Distractors: Customer manages applications on its instances is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is a customer task for EC2, not AWS. AWS maintains the global network and Regions is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: Choose the two AWS capabilities that meet these needs: decide who maintains the global network backbone, Regions, and Availability Zones; and decide who maintains the host hypervisor and the physical servers.
options: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
AWS maintains the global network and Regions
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
Customer configures security groups and firewall rules
Customer manages applications on its instances
explanation: AWS maintains the global network and Regions is correct: AWS maintains the global infrastructure, including Regions, Availability Zones, and the network backbone. AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is correct: AWS is responsible for the infrastructure that runs services, including the hypervisor and physical hardware. Customer configures security groups and firewall rules is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Customer manages applications on its instances is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is a customer task for EC2, not AWS.

skill: Understand the AWS shared responsibility model
question: An operations team wants to decide who manages application code and access on top of a customer's EC2 instance. Which choice is most appropriate?
options: Customer manages applications on its instances
Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system
AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware
AWS provides physical data center security
explanation: Customer manages applications on its instances is correct because The customer manages the applications, code, and access running on its own EC2 instances. Distractors: Customer patches the EC2 guest operating system is a customer task for EC2, not AWS. AWS maintains the hypervisor and physical hardware is an AWS responsibility, not the customer's. AWS provides physical data center security is always an AWS responsibility.

question: During implementation, you must give a specific person long-term credentials to sign in to the account. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: IAM user is correct because An IAM user is an identity with long-term credentials for a person or workload that signs in to the account. Distractors: IAM role provides temporary credentials assumed by services or users. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds a second authentication factor.

question: An organization has the following requirement: let an application on EC2 get temporary credentials to call AWS without stored keys. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: IAM role is correct because An IAM role grants temporary credentials that services or users assume, avoiding long-term keys in code. Distractors: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds a second authentication factor. Root user protection is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once.

question: What should be configured first when the goal is to attach the same permissions to many users at once by collecting them together?
explanation: IAM group is correct because An IAM group is a collection of users so you attach a permission set to many identities at once. Distractors: Least privilege grants only the permissions required. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds a second authentication factor. Root user protection is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: Two requirements must be met: require a second authentication factor so a stolen password alone cannot sign in; and centrally manage workforce single sign-on across many AWS accounts and applications. Select the two correct options.
explanation: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is correct: MFA requires an additional factor beyond the password, strongly protecting accounts including the root user. AWS IAM Identity Center is correct: IAM Identity Center centrally manages workforce access and single sign-on to multiple accounts and applications. IAM user is a long-term identity for a person or workload. IAM role provides temporary credentials assumed by services or users. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once.

question: Your team needs to follow best practice for the AWS account root user. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: Root user protection is correct because Best practice is to secure the root user with MFA, avoid daily use, and not create root access keys. Distractors: Least privilege grants only the permissions required. AWS IAM Identity Center centralizes workforce single sign-on across accounts. IAM policy is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: To grant only the permissions a task needs and nothing more, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Least privilege is correct because Least privilege grants only the permissions required for a task, reducing risk if credentials are misused. Distractors: IAM policy is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon Cognito handles sign-in for end users of an app, not internal AWS access. AWS IAM Identity Center centralizes workforce single sign-on across accounts.

question: A workload requires that you centrally manage workforce single sign-on across many AWS accounts and applications. What is the correct choice?
explanation: AWS IAM Identity Center is correct because IAM Identity Center centrally manages workforce access and single sign-on to multiple accounts and applications. Distractors: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. IAM policy is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon Cognito handles sign-in for end users of an app, not internal AWS access.

question: Pick the two features that address these requirements: define permissions in a JSON document attached to a user, group, or role; and give a specific person long-term credentials to sign in to the account.
explanation: IAM policy is correct: An IAM policy is a JSON document that defines permissions and is attached to identities or resources. IAM user is correct: An IAM user is an identity with long-term credentials for a person or workload that signs in to the account. Least privilege grants only the permissions required. AWS IAM Identity Center centralizes workforce single sign-on across accounts. Amazon Cognito handles sign-in for end users of an app, not internal AWS access.

question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to add sign-up, sign-in, and access control for a customer-facing web or mobile app. What do you select?
explanation: Amazon Cognito is correct because Amazon Cognito provides authentication, authorization, and user management for web and mobile applications. Distractors: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. IAM user is a long-term identity for a person or workload. IAM role provides temporary credentials assumed by services or users.

question: Which AWS feature lets you manage which AWS accounts and services a workforce identity can reach from a central place?
explanation: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is correct because IAM is the service that securely controls access to AWS resources through identities and permissions. Distractors: IAM role provides temporary credentials assumed by services or users. IAM group groups users to attach permissions at once. IAM user is a long-term identity for a person or workload.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Which option should you implement to continuously monitor accounts and workloads for malicious or unauthorized activity using threat intelligence?
explanation: Amazon GuardDuty is correct because GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior. Distractors: AWS Security Hub aggregates security findings. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: You need a solution to automatically scan EC2 instances and container images for software vulnerabilities. Which option fits best?
explanation: Amazon Inspector is correct because Amazon Inspector continuously scans workloads such as EC2 and container images for software vulnerabilities. Distractors: Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code for vulnerabilities. Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3. AWS WAF filters web requests at the application layer.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to discover and protect sensitive data such as personal information stored in Amazon S3. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: Amazon Macie is correct because Macie uses machine learning to discover and classify sensitive data, such as PII, stored in Amazon S3. Distractors: Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code for vulnerabilities. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. AWS Security Hub aggregates security findings.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: see a central, prioritized view of security findings across many AWS accounts; and download AWS compliance reports such as SOC and PCI on demand.
explanation: AWS Security Hub is correct: Security Hub aggregates and prioritizes security findings from multiple services into a single view. AWS Artifact is correct: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS security and compliance reports and agreements. Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code for vulnerabilities. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: A design review identifies this requirement: filter malicious web requests such as SQL injection before they reach an application. What should you configure?
explanation: AWS WAF is correct because AWS WAF is a web application firewall that filters HTTP and HTTPS requests using rules you define. Distractors: AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit, not metrics. AWS Shield protects against DDoS attacks. AWS Artifact provides compliance reports on demand.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to defend an application against distributed denial of service (DDoS) attacks?
explanation: AWS Shield is correct because AWS Shield provides managed DDoS protection, with Standard included and Advanced for enhanced protection. Distractors: AWS Artifact provides compliance reports on demand. AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit, not metrics. AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: An operations team wants to download AWS compliance reports such as SOC and PCI on demand. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: AWS Artifact is correct because AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS security and compliance reports and agreements. Distractors: AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit, not metrics. AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) issues and manages TLS certificates.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: record account activity and API calls so they can be audited later; and continuously monitor accounts and workloads for malicious or unauthorized activity using threat intelligence?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail is correct: CloudTrail records account activity and API calls, providing an audit trail for governance and compliance. Amazon GuardDuty is correct: GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior. AWS Shield protects against DDoS attacks. AWS Artifact provides compliance reports on demand. AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: During implementation, you must get recommendations that check accounts against security best practices and limits. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor is correct because Trusted Advisor inspects an account and recommends improvements, including security best practices. Distractors: Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) issues and manages TLS certificates. Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code for vulnerabilities.

skill: Identify AWS security and compliance services
question: An organization has the following requirement: issue and manage the TLS certificates used to secure websites and applications. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) is correct because ACM provisions and manages public and private TLS certificates for use with AWS services. Distractors: Amazon GuardDuty detects threats, it does not scan code for vulnerabilities. Amazon Inspector scans for software vulnerabilities. Amazon Macie discovers sensitive data in S3.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: What should be configured first when the goal is to create and control the cryptographic keys used to encrypt data across AWS services?
explanation: AWS Key Management Service (KMS) is correct because AWS KMS lets you create and manage encryption keys and control their use across AWS services. Distractors: Encryption at rest is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Encryption in transit (TLS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: Your team needs to protect data stored on disk so it is unreadable without the key. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: Encryption at rest is correct because Encryption at rest protects stored data, for example S3, EBS, and RDS data encrypted with KMS keys. Distractors: AWS Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets. Amazon S3 default encryption (SSE-KMS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Encryption in transit (TLS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: To protect data as it travels between a client and AWS over the network, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Encryption in transit (TLS) is correct because Encryption in transit uses TLS to protect data while it moves across the network. Distractors: AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated hardware security modules. AWS Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets. Amazon S3 default encryption (SSE-KMS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: Choose the two AWS capabilities that meet these needs: store database passwords and API keys securely and rotate them automatically; and keep ownership of your content and decide which services store and process it.
explanation: AWS Secrets Manager is correct: Secrets Manager securely stores and automatically rotates secrets such as database credentials and API keys. Customer ownership of data is correct: Customers retain ownership and control of their content and choose how it is stored, processed, and protected. AWS Key Management Service (KMS) creates and controls encryption keys. Encryption at rest is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Encryption in transit (TLS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: A workload requires that you set a bucket so new objects are encrypted with a KMS key by default. What is the correct choice?
explanation: Amazon S3 default encryption (SSE-KMS) is correct because S3 lets you set a bucket default of SSE-KMS so new objects are encrypted with your chosen KMS key. Distractors: AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated hardware security modules. Customer ownership of data is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) issues and manages TLS certificates.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to manage your own keys in a dedicated hardware security module for compliance. What do you select?
explanation: AWS CloudHSM is correct because AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated, FIPS-validated hardware security modules for managing your own keys. Distractors: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) issues and manages TLS certificates. Amazon EBS encryption is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Customer ownership of data is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: Which AWS feature lets you keep ownership of your content and decide which services store and process it?
explanation: Customer ownership of data is correct because Customers retain ownership and control of their content and choose how it is stored, processed, and protected. Distractors: AWS KMS key rotation is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) issues and manages TLS certificates. Amazon EBS encryption is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: Two requirements must be met: issue the TLS certificate that encrypts traffic to a public website; and create and control the cryptographic keys used to encrypt data across AWS services. Select the two correct options.
explanation: AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) is correct: ACM issues and manages the TLS certificates that enable encryption in transit for applications. AWS Key Management Service (KMS) is correct: AWS KMS lets you create and manage encryption keys and control their use across AWS services. AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated hardware security modules. Customer ownership of data is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon EBS encryption is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: Which option should you implement to automatically encrypt an EBS volume attached to an instance?
explanation: Amazon EBS encryption is correct because EBS encryption uses KMS keys to encrypt volumes, snapshots, and the data moving between instance and volume. Distractors: AWS KMS key rotation is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Key Management Service (KMS) creates and controls encryption keys. Encryption at rest is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

skill: Identify components of AWS data protection
question: You need a solution to rotate the keys used for encryption on a schedule managed by AWS. Which option fits best?
explanation: AWS KMS key rotation is correct because AWS KMS can automatically rotate the cryptographic material of a KMS key on a managed schedule. Distractors: Encryption at rest is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Encryption in transit (TLS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. AWS Key Management Service (KMS) creates and controls encryption keys.

question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to run virtual servers where you control and manage the operating system. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: Amazon EC2 is correct because Amazon EC2 provides resizable virtual machine instances where you control the operating system and software. Distractors: Amazon ECS orchestrates Docker containers. AWS Lambda runs code serverless with no servers to manage. AWS Elastic Beanstalk deploys and manages an app platform for you.

question: A design review identifies this requirement: run code in response to events with no servers to provision and pay only for execution time. What should you configure?
explanation: AWS Lambda is correct because AWS Lambda is serverless compute that runs code on events and bills only for the compute time consumed. Distractors: Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers you manage. Amazon ECS orchestrates Docker containers. AWS Elastic Beanstalk deploys and manages an app platform for you.

question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to deploy a web application and let AWS handle capacity, load balancing, and scaling for you?
explanation: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is correct because Elastic Beanstalk deploys and manages applications, handling provisioning, load balancing, and scaling automatically. Distractors: Amazon ECS orchestrates Docker containers. AWS Fargate runs containers serverless without managing EC2. EC2 Auto Scaling is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: Pick the two features that address these requirements: run and orchestrate Docker containers as a managed AWS service; and spread incoming traffic across multiple healthy targets such as EC2 instances.
explanation: Amazon ECS is correct: Amazon ECS is a managed container orchestration service for running and scaling Docker containers. Elastic Load Balancing is correct: Elastic Load Balancing distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets for availability and fault tolerance. AWS Lambda runs code serverless with no servers to manage. AWS Elastic Beanstalk deploys and manages an app platform for you. AWS Fargate runs containers serverless without managing EC2.

question: An operations team wants to run containers without managing any EC2 servers using a serverless engine. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: AWS Fargate is correct because AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers, so you run tasks without managing EC2 instances. Distractors: Amazon EKS runs managed Kubernetes. Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers you manage. Amazon ECS orchestrates Docker containers.

question: During implementation, you must automatically add or remove EC2 capacity to match demand. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: EC2 Auto Scaling is correct because EC2 Auto Scaling adjusts the number of instances automatically based on demand and scaling policies. Distractors: Elastic Load Balancing is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon EKS runs managed Kubernetes. Amazon Lightsail offers simple low-cost virtual private servers.

question: An organization has the following requirement: spread incoming traffic across multiple healthy targets such as EC2 instances. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: Elastic Load Balancing is correct because Elastic Load Balancing distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets for availability and fault tolerance. Distractors: Amazon EKS runs managed Kubernetes. Amazon Lightsail offers simple low-cost virtual private servers. Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers you manage.

question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: run managed Kubernetes for container orchestration on AWS; and run code in response to events with no servers to provision and pay only for execution time.
explanation: Amazon EKS is correct: Amazon EKS is a managed Kubernetes service for running Kubernetes workloads on AWS. AWS Lambda is correct: AWS Lambda is serverless compute that runs code on events and bills only for the compute time consumed. Elastic Load Balancing is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon Lightsail offers simple low-cost virtual private servers. Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers you manage.

question: What should be configured first when the goal is to launch a simple virtual private server with a predictable monthly price for a small site?
explanation: Amazon Lightsail is correct because Amazon Lightsail offers easy-to-use virtual private servers and bundles at a low, predictable monthly price. Distractors: AWS Elastic Beanstalk deploys and manages an app platform for you. Amazon EC2 provides virtual servers you manage. AWS Lambda runs code serverless with no servers to manage.

question: Your team needs to store and retrieve any amount of object data with very high durability. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: Amazon S3 is correct because Amazon S3 is object storage designed to store and retrieve any amount of data with 11 nines of durability. Distractors: Amazon EBS provides a block volume for a single instance. Amazon EFS provides a shared file system for many instances. Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is lowest-cost archival with hours-long retrieval.

question: To attach a persistent block storage volume to a single EC2 instance, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Amazon EBS is correct because Amazon EBS provides persistent block storage volumes that attach to EC2 instances. Distractors: Amazon EFS provides a shared file system for many instances. Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is lowest-cost archival with hours-long retrieval. Amazon S3 Lifecycle policy moves objects by age or date, not by changing access patterns.

question: A workload requires that you provide a shared file system many Linux EC2 instances can mount at the same time. What is the correct choice?
explanation: Amazon EFS is correct because Amazon EFS is a managed, elastic NFS file system that multiple EC2 instances access concurrently. Distractors: Amazon S3 Lifecycle policy moves objects by age or date, not by changing access patterns. Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering moves objects automatically based on changing access patterns. Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is lowest-cost archival with hours-long retrieval.

question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: archive data at the lowest storage cost where retrieval can take hours; and connect on-premises applications to AWS storage through a hybrid appliance?
explanation: Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is correct: S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the lowest-cost class for long-term archive with retrieval times measured in hours. AWS Storage Gateway is correct: AWS Storage Gateway connects on-premises environments to AWS storage for hybrid use cases. Amazon EFS provides a shared file system for many instances. Amazon S3 Lifecycle policy moves objects by age or date, not by changing access patterns. Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering moves objects automatically based on changing access patterns.

question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to automatically move objects to lower-cost tiers based on a fixed age such as after 90 days. What do you select?
explanation: Amazon S3 Lifecycle policy is correct because An S3 Lifecycle policy transitions or expires objects on a schedule based on object age or date rules. Distractors: Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering moves objects automatically based on changing access patterns. Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is lowest-cost archival with hours-long retrieval. Amazon S3 stores objects, not block volumes for one instance.

question: Which AWS feature lets you automatically optimize cost when object access patterns are unknown or change over time?
explanation: Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering is correct because S3 Intelligent-Tiering monitors access and moves objects between tiers automatically when patterns are unknown or changing. Distractors: Amazon S3 Lifecycle policy moves objects by age or date, not by changing access patterns. Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is lowest-cost archival with hours-long retrieval. Amazon EFS provides a shared file system for many instances.

question: Which option should you implement to connect on-premises applications to AWS storage through a hybrid appliance?
explanation: AWS Storage Gateway is correct because AWS Storage Gateway connects on-premises environments to AWS storage for hybrid use cases. Distractors: Amazon S3 stores objects, not block volumes for one instance. Amazon EBS provides a block volume for a single instance. Amazon FSx is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: Choose the two AWS capabilities that meet these needs: provide fully managed Windows or Lustre file systems on AWS; and provide a shared file system many Linux EC2 instances can mount at the same time.
explanation: Amazon FSx is correct: Amazon FSx provides fully managed third-party file systems such as Windows File Server and Lustre. Amazon EFS is correct: Amazon EFS is a managed, elastic NFS file system that multiple EC2 instances access concurrently. Amazon S3 stores objects, not block volumes for one instance. Amazon EBS provides a block volume for a single instance. Amazon S3 Glacier Deep Archive is lowest-cost archival with hours-long retrieval.

question: You need a solution to create a logically isolated private network with your own IP range and subnets. Which option fits best?
explanation: Amazon VPC is correct because Amazon VPC provisions a logically isolated section of AWS with full control over IP addressing and subnets. Distractors: Amazon Route 53 is DNS and routing. Amazon CloudFront is a content delivery network. AWS Direct Connect is a dedicated private link to AWS.

question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to provide a scalable DNS service that routes users to applications. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: Amazon Route 53 is correct because Route 53 is a highly available DNS web service that routes users to applications and can run health checks. Distractors: Amazon CloudFront is a content delivery network. AWS Direct Connect is a dedicated private link to AWS. AWS Site-to-Site VPN is an encrypted tunnel over the internet.

question: A design review identifies this requirement: cache and deliver content to users worldwide with low latency. What should you configure?
explanation: Amazon CloudFront is correct because CloudFront is a content delivery network that caches content at edge locations for low-latency delivery. Distractors: AWS Site-to-Site VPN is an encrypted tunnel over the internet. Security group is a stateful instance-level firewall. AWS Direct Connect is a dedicated private link to AWS.

question: Two requirements must be met: establish a dedicated private physical connection from a data center to AWS; and reach an AWS service privately from a VPC without using the public internet. Select the two correct options.
explanation: AWS Direct Connect is correct: Direct Connect provides a dedicated, private physical network connection between on-premises and AWS. VPC endpoint is correct: A VPC endpoint privately connects a VPC to supported AWS services without traversing the public internet. Amazon CloudFront is a content delivery network. AWS Site-to-Site VPN is an encrypted tunnel over the internet. Security group is a stateful instance-level firewall.

question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to create an encrypted tunnel over the internet between on-premises and a VPC?
explanation: AWS Site-to-Site VPN is correct because AWS Site-to-Site VPN creates an encrypted IPsec tunnel over the internet between your network and a VPC. Distractors: Security group is a stateful instance-level firewall. VPC endpoint provides private access to AWS services. Network ACL is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: An operations team wants to act as a stateful virtual firewall controlling traffic to an EC2 instance. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: Security group is correct because A security group is a stateful virtual firewall that controls inbound and outbound traffic for instances. Distractors: VPC endpoint provides private access to AWS services. Network ACL is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon VPC is an isolated private network, not a CDN.

question: During implementation, you must reach an AWS service privately from a VPC without using the public internet. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: VPC endpoint is correct because A VPC endpoint privately connects a VPC to supported AWS services without traversing the public internet. Distractors: Amazon VPC is an isolated private network, not a CDN. Amazon Route 53 is DNS and routing. Network ACL is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement.

question: Pick the two features that address these requirements: apply stateless allow and deny rules at the subnet boundary; and cache and deliver content to users worldwide with low latency.
explanation: Network ACL is correct: A network ACL is a stateless, optional layer of security that allows or denies traffic at the subnet level. Amazon CloudFront is correct: CloudFront is a content delivery network that caches content at edge locations for low-latency delivery. Amazon VPC is an isolated private network, not a CDN. Amazon Route 53 is DNS and routing. AWS Direct Connect is a dedicated private link to AWS.

question: An organization has the following requirement: run a managed relational database using a standard engine such as MySQL, PostgreSQL, or SQL Server. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: Amazon RDS is correct because Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service for standard engines, handling provisioning, patching, and backups. Distractors: Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL key-value database. Amazon Redshift is a data warehouse for analytics. Amazon ElastiCache is an in-memory cache.

question: What should be configured first when the goal is to use a fully managed NoSQL key-value database with single-digit millisecond latency at any scale?
explanation: Amazon DynamoDB is correct because DynamoDB is a serverless NoSQL database delivering consistent single-digit millisecond performance at any scale. Distractors: Amazon Aurora is a cloud-built MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible database. Amazon ElastiCache is an in-memory cache. Amazon Redshift is a data warehouse for analytics.

question: Your team needs to run a cloud-built database that is MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible with higher performance. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: Amazon Aurora is correct because Aurora is a cloud-built, MySQL- and PostgreSQL-compatible relational database with improved performance and availability. Distractors: Amazon Redshift is a data warehouse for analytics. Amazon DocumentDB is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL key-value database.

question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: add an in-memory cache in front of a database to speed up reads; and run a managed MongoDB-compatible document database.
explanation: Amazon ElastiCache is correct: ElastiCache provides managed in-memory caching with Redis or Memcached to accelerate read-heavy workloads. Amazon DocumentDB is correct: Amazon DocumentDB is a managed document database that is compatible with MongoDB workloads. Amazon Redshift is a data warehouse for analytics. AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon RDS is a managed relational database for standard engines.

question: To run a petabyte-scale data warehouse for analytics across large datasets, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Amazon Redshift is correct because Amazon Redshift is a managed data warehouse for running analytic queries over very large datasets. Distractors: AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon DocumentDB is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon RDS is a managed relational database for standard engines.

question: A workload requires that you migrate an existing database to AWS while the source keeps running. What is the correct choice?
explanation: AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) is correct because AWS DMS migrates databases to AWS with minimal downtime while the source database remains operational. Distractors: Amazon DocumentDB is a different AWS capability and does not best satisfy this requirement. Amazon RDS is a managed relational database for standard engines. Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL key-value database.

question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to run a managed MongoDB-compatible document database. What do you select?
explanation: Amazon DocumentDB is correct because Amazon DocumentDB is a managed document database that is compatible with MongoDB workloads. Distractors: Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL key-value database. Amazon Aurora is a cloud-built MySQL/PostgreSQL-compatible database. Amazon RDS is a managed relational database for standard engines.

skill: Understand AWS global infrastructure and ways to access AWS
question: Which AWS feature lets you choose the geographic area that contains multiple isolated Availability Zones?
explanation: AWS Region is correct because An AWS Region is a physical geographic area that contains multiple isolated Availability Zones. Distractors: AWS CloudFormation provisions infrastructure as code. Availability Zone is an isolated datacenter group in a Region. Edge location caches content near users.

skill: Understand AWS global infrastructure and ways to access AWS
question: Which option should you implement to spread a workload across isolated data centers within one Region for high availability?
explanation: Availability Zone is correct because Availability Zones are isolated data centers within a Region; spreading across them improves availability. Distractors: Edge location caches content near users. AWS CloudFormation provisions infrastructure as code. AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) runs AWS commands in a terminal.

skill: Understand AWS global infrastructure and ways to access AWS
question: You need a solution to cache content close to end users at points of presence for low latency. Which option fits best?
explanation: Edge location is correct because Edge locations are sites used by CloudFront and other services to cache content closer to users. Distractors: AWS CloudFormation provisions infrastructure as code. AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) runs AWS commands in a terminal. AWS Management Console is the web interface for AWS.

skill: Understand AWS global infrastructure and ways to access AWS
question: Which two options together satisfy these requirements: define and provision AWS resources from declarative templates as code; and choose the geographic area that contains multiple isolated Availability Zones?
explanation: AWS CloudFormation is correct: CloudFormation provisions AWS resources from declarative templates, treating infrastructure as code. AWS Region is correct: An AWS Region is a physical geographic area that contains multiple isolated Availability Zones. Availability Zone is an isolated datacenter group in a Region. Edge location caches content near users. AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) runs AWS commands in a terminal.

skill: Understand AWS global infrastructure and ways to access AWS
question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to control AWS services by typing commands in a terminal or shell. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) is correct because The AWS CLI controls AWS services from a command-line shell and can be scripted for automation. Distractors: Availability Zone is an isolated datacenter group in a Region. AWS Management Console is the web interface for AWS. AWS Region is a geographic area of Availability Zones.

skill: Understand AWS global infrastructure and ways to access AWS
question: A design review identifies this requirement: manage AWS services through a web-based graphical interface in a browser. What should you configure?
explanation: AWS Management Console is correct because The AWS Management Console is the web application for accessing and managing AWS services. Distractors: AWS Region is a geographic area of Availability Zones. Availability Zone is an isolated datacenter group in a Region. Edge location caches content near users.

skill: Identify AWS management and monitoring tools
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to collect metrics and logs and trigger alarms on AWS resources?
explanation: Amazon CloudWatch is correct because CloudWatch monitors resources and applications by collecting metrics and logs and triggering alarms and actions. Distractors: AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit, not metrics. AWS Config records and evaluates resource configurations. AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements.

skill: Identify AWS management and monitoring tools
question: An operations team wants to record API calls and account activity for auditing and governance. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: AWS CloudTrail is correct because CloudTrail records API calls and account activity to support auditing and governance. Distractors: AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements. AWS Systems Manager automates operational tasks across resources. AWS Config records and evaluates resource configurations.

skill: Identify AWS management and monitoring tools
question: During implementation, you must record resource configurations and evaluate them against rules over time. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: AWS Config is correct because AWS Config records resource configurations and evaluates compliance against rules over time. Distractors: AWS Health Dashboard shows service and account health events. AWS Trusted Advisor recommends best-practice improvements. AWS Systems Manager automates operational tasks across resources.

skill: Identify AWS management and monitoring tools
question: Choose the two AWS capabilities that meet these needs: get best-practice recommendations across cost, performance, security, and fault tolerance; and collect metrics and logs and trigger alarms on AWS resources.
explanation: AWS Trusted Advisor is correct: Trusted Advisor inspects an environment and recommends improvements across several categories and service limits. Amazon CloudWatch is correct: CloudWatch monitors resources and applications by collecting metrics and logs and triggering alarms and actions. AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit, not metrics. AWS Config records and evaluates resource configurations. AWS Systems Manager automates operational tasks across resources.

skill: Identify AWS management and monitoring tools
question: An organization has the following requirement: automate operational tasks and run commands across many EC2 instances. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: AWS Systems Manager is correct because AWS Systems Manager provides operational management and automation across AWS resources. Distractors: AWS Health Dashboard shows service and account health events. Amazon CloudWatch collects metrics, logs, and alarms. AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit, not metrics.

skill: Identify AWS management and monitoring tools
question: What should be configured first when the goal is to see the status of AWS services and events that affect your specific account?
explanation: AWS Health Dashboard is correct because The AWS Health Dashboard shows AWS service status and personalized events affecting your resources. Distractors: AWS CloudTrail records API calls for audit, not metrics. AWS Config records and evaluates resource configurations. Amazon CloudWatch collects metrics, logs, and alarms.

question: Your team needs to pay for EC2 by the second with no commitment and no risk that AWS reclaims the capacity. Which AWS option should you use?
explanation: On-Demand pricing is correct because On-Demand bills per second with no commitment and is not interrupted, suiting short-term or unpredictable workloads. Distractors: Spot Instances are cheapest but can be interrupted. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend.

question: To commit to a specific instance configuration for one or three years to get a large discount, which AWS capability should you choose?
explanation: Reserved Instances is correct because Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration in exchange for a one- or three-year commitment. Distractors: On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend. Spot Instances are cheapest but can be interrupted.

question: A workload requires that you commit to a consistent amount of compute spend per hour for one or three years for a flexible discount. What is the correct choice?
explanation: Savings Plans is correct because Savings Plans give lower prices for committing to a consistent dollars-per-hour amount of usage for one or three years. Distractors: On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Spot Instances are cheapest but can be interrupted.

question: Two requirements must be met: use spare EC2 capacity at a steep discount for workloads that tolerate interruption; and pay for EC2 by the second with no commitment and no risk that AWS reclaims the capacity. Select the two correct options.
explanation: Spot Instances is correct: Spot Instances use spare capacity at up to 90 percent off but can be interrupted, suiting fault-tolerant workloads. On-Demand pricing is correct: On-Demand bills per second with no commitment and is not interrupted, suiting short-term or unpredictable workloads. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend. Volume-based (tiered) pricing lowers per-unit price as usage grows.

question: As the AWS administrator, you are asked to pay less per gigabyte as monthly storage usage in a service such as S3 grows. What do you select?
options: AWS Free Tier (credits and account plans)
explanation: Volume-based (tiered) pricing is correct because Some services charge less per unit as usage grows, so higher consumption earns lower per-unit pricing. Distractors: Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. AWS Free Tier (credits and account plans) offers sign-up credits and free services for exploration. On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price.

question: Which AWS feature lets you explore AWS at no cost using sign-up credits and always-free services?
options: AWS Free Tier (credits and account plans)
explanation: AWS Free Tier (credits and account plans) is correct because New AWS accounts receive sign-up credits plus always-free services and can choose a Free or Paid account plan. Distractors: On-Demand pricing has no commitment but the highest unit price. Reserved Instances discount a specific instance configuration for a 1- or 3-year term. Savings Plans discount a committed dollars-per-hour spend.

question: Which option should you implement to visualize, understand, and forecast AWS spend and usage over time?
explanation: AWS Cost Explorer is correct because Cost Explorer lets you visualize, understand, and forecast AWS costs and usage over time. Distractors: AWS Budgets alerts when spend or usage crosses a threshold. AWS Cost and Usage Report gives the most detailed billing data. AWS Organizations consolidated billing combines billing across accounts.

question: You need a solution to set a cost or usage threshold and be alerted when it is exceeded. Which option fits best?
explanation: AWS Budgets is correct because AWS Budgets sets custom cost and usage budgets and alerts you when thresholds are exceeded. Distractors: AWS Organizations consolidated billing combines billing across accounts. Cost allocation tags categorize costs by label. AWS Cost and Usage Report gives the most detailed billing data.

question: You are administering an AWS environment and need to get the most detailed, line-item billing data for deep analysis. Which option best meets the requirement?
explanation: AWS Cost and Usage Report is correct because The AWS Cost and Usage Report provides the most comprehensive, line-item billing and usage data. Distractors: AWS Cost Explorer visualizes and forecasts spend. AWS Organizations consolidated billing combines billing across accounts. Cost allocation tags categorize costs by label.

question: Pick the two features that address these requirements: combine billing across many accounts and share volume discounts; and set a cost or usage threshold and be alerted when it is exceeded.
explanation: AWS Organizations consolidated billing is correct: Consolidated billing in AWS Organizations combines account usage into one bill with shared volume pricing. AWS Budgets is correct: AWS Budgets sets custom cost and usage budgets and alerts you when thresholds are exceeded. Cost allocation tags categorize costs by label. AWS Cost Explorer visualizes and forecasts spend. AWS Cost and Usage Report gives the most detailed billing data.

question: A design review identifies this requirement: categorize and track costs by project or team using labels on resources. What should you configure?
explanation: Cost allocation tags is correct because Cost allocation tags let you categorize and track AWS costs by labels such as project, team, or environment. Distractors: AWS Cost Explorer visualizes and forecasts spend. AWS Budgets alerts when spend or usage crosses a threshold. AWS Cost and Usage Report gives the most detailed billing data.

skill: Identify AWS technical resources and support options
question: Which AWS capability is the best fit when the requirement is to use the no-cost plan that covers account and billing questions and core Trusted Advisor checks?
explanation: AWS Basic Support is correct because Basic Support is included for every account and covers account and billing help, service health, and core Trusted Advisor checks. Distractors: AWS Enterprise Support adds a Technical Account Manager and fastest response. AWS documentation and whitepapers are free self-service resources, not a support plan. AWS Business Support+ adds 24x7 engineers, 500+ checks, and AI help.

skill: Identify AWS technical resources and support options
question: An operations team wants to get 24x7 access to Cloud Support Engineers, over 500 Trusted Advisor checks, and AI-powered help. Which choice is most appropriate?
explanation: AWS Business Support+ is correct because Business Support+ adds 24x7 access to Cloud Support Engineers, more than 500 Trusted Advisor checks, the Support API, and AI-powered assistance. Distractors: AWS re:Post is a community Q and A service. AWS Basic Support is free with core checks only. AWS Enterprise Support adds a Technical Account Manager and fastest response.

skill: Identify AWS technical resources and support options
question: During implementation, you must get a designated Technical Account Manager and the fastest response for business-critical workloads. Which AWS feature should you choose?
explanation: AWS Enterprise Support is correct because Enterprise Support adds a designated Technical Account Manager and the fastest response times for business- and mission-critical workloads. Distractors: AWS Basic Support is free with core checks only. AWS Business Support+ adds 24x7 engineers, 500+ checks, and AI help. AWS re:Post is a community Q and A service.

skill: Identify AWS technical resources and support options
question: Select the two configurations that best support these requirements: find free best-practice guidance, technical papers, and documentation published by AWS; and get 24x7 access to Cloud Support Engineers, over 500 Trusted Advisor checks, and AI-powered help.
explanation: AWS documentation and whitepapers is correct: AWS publishes free documentation, technical papers, and best-practice guides as technical resources. AWS Business Support+ is correct: Business Support+ adds 24x7 access to Cloud Support Engineers, more than 500 Trusted Advisor checks, the Support API, and AI-powered assistance. AWS re:Post is a community Q and A service. AWS Basic Support is free with core checks only. AWS Enterprise Support adds a Technical Account Manager and fastest response.

skill: Identify AWS technical resources and support options
question: An organization has the following requirement: ask technical questions and get community and AWS answers in a managed Q and A service. What is the least-complex correct option?
explanation: AWS re:Post is correct because AWS re:Post is a community question-and-answer service for technical guidance, replacing the older forums. Distractors: AWS Business Support+ adds 24x7 engineers, 500+ checks, and AI help. AWS Enterprise Support adds a Technical Account Manager and fastest response. AWS Basic Support is free with core checks only.
